text: Handling deadlines effectively is a key skill in both work and study. Good time management helps you plan your tasks realistically, breaking large projects into smaller steps. Prioritization is equally important—focus first on urgent or high-impact tasks to avoid last-minute stress. Scope management means understanding your limits and not taking on too much at once; if a project becomes too large, it’s better to adjust expectations early. Finally, clear communication with team members or supervisors is crucial. If you foresee a delay, explain the situation and suggest a revised timeline. By combining these strategies, you can meet deadlines with less anxiety and greater success.
